method: 3D triadic bridge, explicit three-level authority routing
model: Full 3D H8 elasticity with PZT eigenstrain, bond and mirror

summary.emergent_statement: A 3D reduced model is stable only when hidden volume physics, interface transmission, and global mirror observables close simultaneously; each failing level owns a different enrichment action.

rows.description: Nominal square stack and mirror across 3D coupled modes
Rectangular mirror splits x/y bending families
Soft asymmetric bond creates shear-localized interface physics
Thin mirror with stiffness gradient creates tilt and torsion
Asymmetric PZT stiffness excites non-axisymmetric response
